### Cool Stuff & Try This
**[OpenAI's Next Big Thing? Hints of a Super-Smart Omni Model: The Decoder]**
OpenAI might be working on a new "omni" model, which is an upgrade to their AI that handles multiple types of input like text, images, and sound all at once – think of it as evolving from a text-only chatbot to a full multimedia helper. It's cool because it could make AI even better at real-world tasks, like describing a photo you upload or generating audio from your ideas, opening up creative
sections.quick_bits:
### Quick Bits
**[UK's New AI Fund Boosts Homegrown Tech]**
The UK is launching a sovereign AI fund with £500 million to build its own computing power for AI, meaning less reliance on other countries' tech – it's cool because it could lead to more accessible AI tools made right at home, like faster apps for education or games without global delays.
Source: https://www.artificialintelligence-news.com/news/uk-sovereign-ai-fund-build-domestic-computing-infrastructure/

### Cool Stuff & Try This
**[Chat with AI to Edit Your Photos Like Magic: The Verge]**
Adobe just launched a public beta of an AI assistant inside Photoshop that lets you describe changes to images in plain words, like "make the sky brighter and add a rainbow," and it does the editing for you. This is exciting because it turns pro-level photo tweaking—stuff that used to need hours of learning—into something as easy as texting a friend, perfect for teens making memes, students jazzing up project
sections.quick_bits:
### Quick Bits
**[AI Agents Get Their Own Social Network]**
Meta just bought Moltbook, a platform like Reddit but for AI agents to post, comment, and connect in an always-on directory—imagine AI helpers chatting and sharing tips like users on TikTok. This is cool because it hints at a future where AIs team up to solve problems for us, maybe helping with school research or game strategies without you lifting a finger.
Source: https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/892178/meta-moltboo
